description: Business Analyst prompt pack — 5 prompts for BAs and requirements professionals

prompts.title: Requirements Document
prompts.desc: Draft a structured requirements document from stakeholder notes.
prompts.prompt:
[ROLE]    You are a senior Business Analyst.
[TASK]    Draft a requirements document based on the stakeholder input or meeting notes below.
[STYLE]   Formal, precise, unambiguous.
[FORMAT]  Sections: Business Objective, Scope, Functional Requirements (numbered), Non-Functional Requirements, Assumptions, Out of Scope.
[INPUT]

prompts.title: Process Flow Summary
prompts.desc: Structure a business process from notes into a clear flow.
prompts.prompt:
[ROLE]    You are a senior Business Analyst.
[TASK]    Summarise and structure the business process described below into a clear process flow.
[STYLE]   Logical, step-by-step, easy to follow.
[FORMAT]  Numbered process steps with decision points noted. Flag any gaps or ambiguities at the end.
[INPUT]

prompts.title: Gap Analysis
prompts.desc: Identify gaps between current state and desired future state.
prompts.prompt:
[ROLE]    You are a senior Business Analyst.
[TASK]    Perform a gap analysis based on the current and desired state described below.
[STYLE]   Analytical, structured, neutral.
[FORMAT]  Table with columns: Area, Current State, Desired State, Gap, Recommended Action.
[INPUT]

prompts.title: Use Case Description
prompts.desc: Write a formal use case from a feature or scenario.
prompts.prompt:
[ROLE]    You are a senior Business Analyst.
[TASK]    Write a formal use case description for the scenario below.
[STYLE]   Precise and unambiguous.
[FORMAT]  Sections: Use Case Name, Actor(s), Preconditions, Main Flow (numbered), Alternative Flows, Postconditions.
[INPUT]

prompts.title: Business Case Summary
prompts.desc: Write a concise business case from project or initiative notes.
prompts.prompt:
[ROLE]    You are a senior Business Analyst.
[TASK]    Write a concise business case for the initiative or project described below.
[STYLE]   Professional, evidence-based, persuasive without being salesy.
[FORMAT]  Sections: Executive Summary, Problem Statement, Proposed Solution, Expected Benefits, Risks, Recommendation.
[INPUT]
